```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm> // for trim

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Read the UserProfile records
  auto result = sqlgen::read<std::vector<UserProfile>>(conn);

  // Check for errors and transform the data
  if (result) {
    std::vector<ValidatedProfile> validated_profiles;
    for (const auto& profile : *result) {
      // Trim the name and email
      std::string trimmed_name = std::ranges::views::trim(profile.name);
      std::string trimmed_email = std::ranges::views::trim(profile.email);

      // Compute the lengths
      int name_length = trimmed_name.length();
      int email_length = trimmed_email.length();

      validated_profiles.push_back(
          ValidatedProfile{
              .user_id = profile.user_id,
              .trimmed_name = std::move(trimmed_name),
              .trimmed_email = std::move(trimmed_email),
              .name_length = name_length,
              .email_length = email_length
          });
    }
    return validated_profiles;
```
```cpp
  } else {
    // Handle error? The problem does not specify, so we return an empty vector.
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>

namespace update_set_operations {

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;
  double balance;
  std::string status;
};

bool update_set_operations() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create the table by writing an empty vector
  std::vector<Account> empty_accounts;
  sqlgen::write(conn, empty_accounts);

  // Insert two initial rows
  std::vector<Account> initial_accounts = {

    {2, 0.0, "inactive"}
  };
  sqlgen::write(conn, initial_accounts);

  // Update the first account (id=1) to set balance to 100.0
  auto update1 = sqlgen::update<Account>(sqlgen::col<"balance">.set(100.0)) | sqlgen::where(sqlgen::col<"account_id"> == 1);
  update1(conn);

  // Update the second account (id=2) to set status to "active"
  auto update2 = sqlgen::update<Account>(sqlgen::col<"status">.set("active")) | sqlgen::where(sqlgen::col<"account_id"> == 2);
  update2(conn);

        return false;
      }
    }
  }
  if (!found1) {
    return false;
  }

  return true;
}

}  // namespace update_set_operations
```
